user_query:
I just chaired our Lichen Working Group meeting and need clean outputs for our records and follow‑ups. Please use the files under input/ and produce the deliverables below under the output/ folder.

Inputs you must use:
- input/meeting_notes_raw.md (meeting notes from 2024-11-12)
- input/action_items.csv (all action items discussed across meetings)
- input/draft_emails.txt (two rough follow-up drafts to participants)
- tools/clean_notes.py (a small helper to scan the notes for issues)

Deliverables:
1) Run the notes quality scan and save its output:
- Execute: python3 tools/clean_notes.py input/meeting_notes_raw.md
- Capture both stdout and stderr into output/logs/clean_notes.log (create the logs directory if needed). This log will be used to interpret warnings.

2) Create a concise meeting summary at output/summary.md with these sections:
- Attendees: list the attendees exactly as listed under the Attendees section in input/meeting_notes_raw.md, in the same order.
- Decisions: extract up to five concrete decisions from the notes, written clearly and briefly (one sentence each).
- Ranked Action Items: build this from input/action_items.csv by applying all of the following rules:
  • Include only rows where status is Open, category is either "Lichen taxonomy" or "Herbarium curation", and due_date is between 2024-11-01 and 2024-12-31 inclusive.
  • Sort by priority (High before Medium before Low), then by due_date ascending, then by id ascending.
  • List every included item with these fields: id, topic, assigned_to, due_date (YYYY-MM-DD), priority.
  • For each listed item, add a flag mentioned_in_notes: yes/no by checking whether the id string (e.g., "[AI-003]") occurs anywhere in input/meeting_notes_raw.md. If yes, also include the line number of the first occurrence from the notes.
- Data Quality Notes: read output/logs/clean_notes.log and summarize any WARNING lines (quote each warning message once and add a one-sentence interpretation of what it implies for the notes or summary).

3) Produce rewritten follow-up emails at output/follow_up_emails.txt by transforming the two drafts in input/draft_emails.txt. For each recipient:
- Rewrite for professional tone, clarity, and brevity (≤120 words per email), and provide a clear subject.
- Ensure the body includes the single highest-priority Open action item assigned to that recipient from input/action_items.csv. If multiple items have the same highest priority for that person, use the one with the earliest due_date. Explicitly include that item’s id and due_date in the email.

4) Save the filtered and sorted action items you used for the Ranked Action Items section as a CSV at output/data/filtered_action_items.csv with the columns: id,topic,category,assigned_to,priority,due_date,status. The row order must match the ranking described above.

Notes and constraints:
- Do not invent attendees, decisions, or action items beyond what is supported by the provided files.
- The ranking must follow the exact priority order High > Medium > Low.
- The summary and emails should reflect the actual meeting content and assignments (e.g., if an item is mentioned in the notes, the mentioned_in_notes flag must say yes with the correct line number).
- All paths are relative to the workspace root. Create any missing output directories as needed. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
